Last updated: 2026-06-07

Checks: 7 0

Knit directory: Breast Cancer Data Analysis/

This reproducible R Markdown analysis was created with workflowr (version 1.7.2). The Checks tab describes the reproducibility checks that were applied when the results were created. The Past versions tab lists the development history.

R Markdown file: up-to-date

Great! Since the R Markdown file has been committed to the Git repository, you know the exact version of the code that produced these results.

Environment: empty

Great job! The global environment was empty. Objects defined in the global environment can affect the analysis in your R Markdown file in unknown ways. For reproduciblity it’s best to always run the code in an empty environment.

Seed: set.seed(20260607)

The command set.seed(20260607) was run prior to running the code in the R Markdown file. Setting a seed ensures that any results that rely on randomness, e.g. subsampling or permutations, are reproducible.

Session information: recorded

Great job! Recording the operating system, R version, and package versions is critical for reproducibility.

Cache: none

Nice! There were no cached chunks for this analysis, so you can be confident that you successfully produced the results during this run.

File paths: relative

Great job! Using relative paths to the files within your workflowr project makes it easier to run your code on other machines.

Repository version: c1c34c9

Great! You are using Git for version control. Tracking code development and connecting the code version to the results is critical for reproducibility.

The results in this page were generated with repository version c1c34c9. See the Past versions tab to see a history of the changes made to the R Markdown and HTML files.

Note that you need to be careful to ensure that all relevant files for the analysis have been committed to Git prior to generating the results (you can use wflow_publish or wflow_git_commit). workflowr only checks the R Markdown file, but you know if there are other scripts or data files that it depends on. Below is the status of the Git repository when the results were generated: 


Untracked files:
    Untracked:  data_clinical_patient.txt

Note that any generated files, e.g. HTML, png, CSS, etc., are not included in this status report because it is ok for generated content to have uncommitted changes.

These are the previous versions of the repository in which changes were made to the R Markdown (analysis/index.Rmd) and HTML (docs/index.html) files. If you’ve configured a remote Git repository (see ?wflow_git_remote), click on the hyperlinks in the table below to view the files as they were in that past version.

File Version Author Date Message

html fbc2be1 Anson Li 2026-06-07 Build site.

html 4d9c0e7 Anson Li 2026-06-07 Build site.

Rmd 584a18f Anson Li 2026-06-07 Test second task and use %in% operator for Task 1

Rmd 304ab83 Anson Li 2026-06-07 Test first task
Rmd 5efd452 Anson Li 2026-06-07 Start workflowr project. 
dataframe <- read.delim("data_clinical_patient.txt", comment.char="#")
# Task 1: filter out so only Stage 3 and 4, then count
number <- dataframe %>%
  filter(AJCC_PATHOLOGIC_TUMOR_STAGE %in% c("Stage III", "Stage IIIA", "Stage IIIB", "Stage IIIC", "Stage IV"))  %>% 
  count()
number
    n
1 269
# Task 2: count patients by cancer subtype, sorted from most to least common
result <- dataframe %>% 
  group_by(HISTOLOGICAL_DIAGNOSIS) %>% 
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count))
result
# A tibble: 9 × 2
  HISTOLOGICAL_DIAGNOSIS           count
  <chr>                            <int>
1 Infiltrating Ductal Carcinoma      784
2 Infiltrating Lobular Carcinoma     203
3 Other, specify                      46
4 Mixed Histology (please specify)    30
5 Mucinous Carcinoma                  17
6 Metaplastic Carcinoma                9
7 Medullary Carcinoma                  6
8 Infiltrating Carcinoma NOS           1
9 [Not Available]                      1
# Task 3: convert months to years by creating new column
dataframe$OS_MONTHS <- as.numeric(dataframe$OS_MONTHS)
Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
result <- dataframe %>%
  mutate(OS_YEARS = OS_MONTHS / 12)
result

# Task 4: plot histogram of patient age, add vertical line at mean age
dataframe$AGE <- as.numeric(dataframe$AGE)
Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
mean_age <- mean(dataframe$AGE, na.rm = TRUE)
ggplot(dataframe, aes(x = AGE)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean_age) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Patient Age", x = "Age", y = "Count")
Warning: Removed 1 row containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

# Task 5: bar chart of patient counts by cancer stage
ggplot(dataframe, aes(x = AJCC_PATHOLOGIC_TUMOR_STAGE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Patient Counts by Cancer Stage", x = "Cancer Stage", y = "Count")

# Task 6: scatterplot of age vs survival years colored by subtype
ggplot(result, aes(x=AGE, y=OS_YEARS, color=HISTOLOGICAL_DIAGNOSIS)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Age vs Survival Years by Cancer Subtype", x = "Age", y = "Survival Years") +
  theme_minimal()
Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).
